Verifying that the vote cast on EVM is r: Verifying that the vote cast on EVM is recorded correctly
Immediately when the election schedule i: Immediately when the election schedule is announced
The Election Commission is an autonomous: The Election Commission is an autonomous constitutional authority responsible for administering election processes.
The voting age was lowered from 21 to 18: The voting age was lowered from 21 to 18 years by the 61st Amendment Act.
EVM stands for Electronic Voting Machine: EVM stands for Electronic Voting Machine, which replaced paper ballot systems.
NOTA (None of the Above) gives voters th: NOTA (None of the Above) gives voters the right to reject all candidates in their constituency.
While other documents may be accepted, V: While other documents may be accepted, Voter ID (EPIC - Electors Photo Identity Card) is the primary document.
In the rare event of a tie, the Returnin: In the rare event of a tie, the Returning Officer decides the winner by drawing lots.
A country is divided into territorial co: A country is divided into territorial constituencies, each electing one representative.
A person confined in prison cannot vote,: A person confined in prison cannot vote, but a person under preventive detention can.
Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail (VVPA: Voter Verifiable Paper Audit Trail (VVPAT) helps voters verify their vote was recorded as cast.
It comes into force immediately upon the: It comes into force immediately upon the announcement of the election schedule by the Election Commission.
Ensure your name is on the electoral rol: Ensure your name is on the electoral roll. You can apply using Form 6 online or offline.
The Election Commission announces the da: The Election Commission announces the dates. The Model Code of Conduct comes into effect immediately.
Candidates file their nomination papers,: Candidates file their nomination papers, which are then scrutinized.
Political parties campaign to win over v: Political parties campaign to win over voters. This ends 48 hours before polling begins.
Registered voters cast their vote at des: Registered voters cast their vote at designated polling stations using EVMs.
EVMs are opened under tight security, vo: EVMs are opened under tight security, votes are counted, and results are declared.
Get your name on the official voter list: Get your name on the official voter list using Form 6.
Election dates are announced and strict: Election dates are announced and strict rules apply to all parties.
People who want to be leaders submit the: People who want to be leaders submit their paperwork.
Parties try to win your vote. This stops: Parties try to win your vote. This stops 2 days before voting day.
You go to the polling booth and press th: You go to the polling booth and press the button on the machine to vote.
Votes are counted and the winners are an: Votes are counted and the winners are announced.
My single vote doesn't matter anyway.
I can't vote because I'm away from my ho: I can't vote because I'm away from my home constituency.
Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) can be: Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs) can be easily hacked.
If my name isn't on the voter slip given: If my name isn't on the voter slip given by political parties, I can't vote.
NOTA (None of the Above) is a wasted vot: NOTA (None of the Above) is a wasted vote.
Many elections have been decided by incr: Many elections have been decided by incredibly small margins — sometimes even 1 or 2 votes! Every single vote shapes the future.
You must vote where you are registered. If you moved, you must update your voter registration to your current address using Form 8.
EVMs are standalone, non-networked machi: EVMs are standalone, non-networked machines with stringent physical and technical security protocols to prevent tampering.
Your name MUST be on the official Electo: Your name MUST be on the official Electoral Roll. A slip from a party does not guarantee your right to vote if you aren't on the roll.
NOTA allows voters to express their disc: NOTA allows voters to express their discontent with all candidates. High NOTA votes send a strong statistical message to political parties.
Present your Voter ID (EPIC) or an appro: Present your Voter ID (EPIC) or an approved ID document to the First Polling Officer. They will verify your identity.
The Second Polling Officer will mark you: The Second Polling Officer will mark your left forefinger with indelible ink to prevent duplicate voting.
You will sign or provide a thumb impress: You will sign or provide a thumb impression in the electoral register (Form 17A).
You'll receive a slip reflecting your se: You'll receive a slip reflecting your serial number. Hand this to the Third Polling Officer.
Proceed to the voting compartment. Only: Proceed to the voting compartment. Only you are allowed inside to ensure ballot secrecy.
Press the blue button on the Electronic: Press the blue button on the Electronic Voting Machine (EVM) next to your chosen candidate or NOTA.
Check the VVPAT machine window. A printe: Check the VVPAT machine window. A printed slip showing your candidate's name will appear for 7 seconds, then drop into the box.
You have successfully voted! Exit the po: You have successfully voted! Exit the polling station peacefully.

What if I don't have a Voter ID?: What if I don't have a Voter ID?
You can register to vote by filling out: You can register to vote by filling out Form 6 online via the Voter Portal (voters.eci.gov.in) or offline through your local Booth Level Officer.
